mnemonics: SNAP - Scalable, Native, Automated, Partitioned (for cloud-native app principles)
LAW - Loose coupling, Asynchronous processing, Write for failure (for design principles)
keyPoints: Microservices architecture decomposes an application into small, independent services that communicate via APIs.
Serverless computing (e.g., AWS Lambda, Azure Functions) abstracts server management, running code in response to events.
Containers (e.g., Docker) and orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes) provide a portable and scalable way to package and run applications.
commonMistakes: Assuming the cloud provider is solely responsible for all security, including application-level security (Shared Responsibility Model).
Designing a tightly coupled architecture that cannot scale independently, creating bottlenecks.
Not designing for failure; assuming underlying infrastructure, services, and networks will always be available.
